reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider that AOD-9604 sits at a use-case crossroads in the expert canon: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:03:55.760) calls it "probably the most number one overrated peptide" for standalone fat loss, while Trevor (qPILtY6QBuo 00:14:43.920) reframes it as a cartilage-regeneration conductor and (JJZzjJhP2rQ 00:13:16.880) says flatly "AOD is not for fat loss. It's for condondraight stimulation." If the goal is fat loss, we'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh whether tesamorelin or a GLP-1 is a better match (Hunter ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:57.800: "I would use tesamorelin and or a GLP-1 agonist"); if the goal is joint/cartilage regeneration, AOD's strongest indication, the operative dose frame is Hunter's 2023 baseline_protocol "300 mcg" AM SubQ "5 days on, 2 days off," "8 weeks on, 8 weeks off," with Taylor (rc66QuY9Drs 00:14:08.639) confirming "300 micrograms 5 days a week in the morning subcutaneously."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Indication confusion (the central caveat): Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:03:55.760) "probably the most number one overrated peptide" for fat loss; Trevor (JJZzjJhP2rQ 00:12:05.920) "AODD9604. It doesn't do anything for fat loss compared to samarellin. Not even in the same world"; Trevor (JJZzjJhP2rQ 00:13:16.880) "AOD is not for fat loss. It's for condondraight stimulation."
- Standalone vs stack: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:46.960) "if you're just going to inject AOD without any of the other things, I'm just being honest with you, it's probably not going to do much"; Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:38.920) frames AOD as an "extra 5% boost" only when stacked with GLP-1 + GH peptide + MOTS-c or SS-31.
- Half-life and dosing-frequency hedge: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:02.600) "AOD9604 also has a very short half-life and dosing protocol were likely never optimized" and (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:08.840) "because of the half-life, you probably need to dose it two or three times a day to really see the effect" — "lifestyle prohibitive."
- Reconstitution gel issue: Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14) "Can acetic acid water prevent AOD 9604 from gelling? Yes. If AOD 9604 gels in bacteriostatic water, using acetic acid water instead can help prevent this."
- Injection-site reaction: Hunter email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df (2025-02-14) "Why do AOD 9604 injections cause bruising/lumps? It's an inflammatory response to subQ injections. Switch to IM (intramuscular) administration."
- Cycling tension: Trevor (qPILtY6QBuo 00:06:59.840) "It doesn't have any receptor downregulation" and (qPILtY6QBuo 00:07:13.599) "more like a daily tap on the door than a wrecking ball" but (qPILtY6QBuo 00:07:20.560) "pragmatically, cycling any intervention is smart" and (qPILtY6QBuo 00:07:30.800) suggests "probably 8 to 12 weeks, four to six weeks off."
- Trial backstory: Hunter email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d (2026-04-07) "Phase 2B trial (500+ participants by Metabolic Pharmaceuticals): statistically significant but barely noticeable real fat loss" and "Company abandoned the program."
- Hunter's dose ceiling self-experiment: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:19.000) "I've taken this as high as 1 mg per day" and (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:20.800) "I know a lot of people only do like 200 micrograms to 300 micrograms."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three experts agree on the dose floor and zero serious-harm signal. Hunter 2023 baseline_protocol lists "Dose" "300 mcg," "Timing" "AM," "Frequency" "5 days on, 2 days off," "Duration" "8 weeks on, 8 weeks off." Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:20.800) confirms "a lot of people only do like 200 micrograms to 300 micrograms." Taylor (rc66QuY9Drs 00:14:08.639) lands at the same number: "300 micrograms 5 days a week in the morning subcutaneously," "Monday through Friday and then I typically take the weekends off." Taylor (L331y2xrWwE 00:13:47.079) adds "8 weeks on 8 weeks off." Safety: Trevor (qPILtY6QBuo 00:19:25.280) "There's no research, not a single published study demonstrating any serious adverse or harmful effects from AOD9604 in humans at any therapeutic dose. Its safety profile is so exceptionally clean. No hepotoxicity, no nephrotoxicity, no impact on fasting glucose or insulin." Mechanism overlap: Trevor (qPILtY6QBuo 00:05:08.080) "AODD9604 is this precision lock pick. It's designed to fit pretty much exclusively in the beta 3 adonuric receptor… on the fat cells" and "activates HSL hormone sensitive lipes" — Hunter and Taylor agree it isolates HGH's fat-burning fragment without blood sugar / muscle complications.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Primary indication disagreement is the headline conflict. Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:03:50.000) frames it as a fat-loss peptide "I've used AOD9604. I don't even necessarily dislike AOD9604. I will say it is probably the most number one overrated peptide." Hunter's Phase-2B-trial position (email threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d 2026-04-07): "Standalone = does NOT move the needle" / "Pushed up to 1 mg/day" / "Stack on top of GLP-1 + GH peptide + MOTS-c → maybe 5% extra boost." Taylor (L331y2xrWwE 00:14:50.639) treats AOD as a real fat-burner: "aod is a growth hormone peptide that is designed to Target burning fat" and (L331y2xrWwE 00:15:12.360) "aod is a super beneficial peptide." Trevor (qPILtY6QBuo 00:14:47.040) reframes the entire use case: "AOD9604 is like the the the guide the conductor. It wakes up the condraittes" — i.e. cartilage regeneration, not fat loss; (JJZzjJhP2rQ 00:12:05.920) "AODD9604. It doesn't do anything for fat loss compared to samarellin" and (JJZzjJhP2rQ 00:13:16.880) "AOD is not for fat loss. It's for condondraight stimulation." Cycling differs: Trevor (qPILtY6QBuo 00:06:59.840) "It doesn't have any receptor downregulation" — implying cycling not strictly required — but Hunter 2023 baseline still lists "8 weeks on, 8 weeks off" and Taylor (L331y2xrWwE 00:13:47.079) lists "8 weeks on 8 weeks off." Route on standalone fat-loss use: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:46.960) frames standalone subQ as low-yield, while Taylor (rc66QuY9Drs 00:14:08.639) uses standalone SubQ daily. Newer-overrides-older: Hunter's 2026-04-07 "#1 overrated" position (threadId 19d6a0b72a24135d) updates his 2023 baseline_protocol — the 2023 protocol is still what he prescribes when AOD is used, but his updated stance is to consider tesamorelin or GLP-1 first.
